calendar.matters:
Petition For Division Of Trust And For Instructions To Trustee
Off Calendar Nov-27-2017 Advanced From Dec-27-17 To Dec-13-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Division Of Trust And For Instructions To Trustee
Off Calendar Dec-14-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ellen Kahn for Petitioner.

Ms. Kahn stated that the Court has the power under 15412 to divide the Trust for good cause, that her Client is the income beneficiary of the Trust, that her Client will transfer a portion to the remainder charitable beneficiaries, that the Trust holds 95 million and that they would like to transfer 8 million. Ms. Kahn also explained that there is only one trust today, that there will be two trust (the main trust and the contributed trust), that they will be governed by the same instrument, that there will be no new instrument and that her Client is the beneficiary and the Trustee of both trust. In addition, Ms. Kahn explained that Ms. Stupski has named The Stupski Foundation as Successor Trustee, that when the Trustee, the remainder beneficiary and the income beneficiary are all the same, it all goes away, and that once the assets are in the new trust and trust accounts, Ms. Stupksi will assign her interest in the new trust to the foundation under the doctrine of merger. Ms. Kahn also spoke of the benefit to the charity receiving funds early, the tax benefit to her Client, the history and generosity of the Stupski's, Mr. Stupski also doing this during his lifetime and the urgency regarding completion of this task by the end of the year. The Petition for Division of Trust and for Instructions to Trustee is taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Ex Parte Petition To Advance Hearing Date And Shorten Time For Notice
Off Calendar Nov-27-2017 Granted Per Written Order
